what_it_does: This file is a ledger metadata stub for an image asset named p-31 in the design-source channel; it references a JPEG blob, records provenance fields, and labels the asset as design ground-truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id recent-pitches/p-31 and address image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-31
mime is image/jpeg and the asset is referenced via blob://b2:440b083cbae0be6187ee81fb287367fd
width and height are both recorded as 0
source is design_source_material and author_session is ch-3mpkjg3r
body text reads 'SOURCE design ground-truth — recent-pitches/p-31.jpg'
standouts: Width and height are 0 despite the file representing a JPEG image
The asset is explicitly tagged as SOURCE design ground-truth
Only a blob reference is provided with no descriptive caption or relationships
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for ledger metadata with id, address, channel, path, blob, mime, source, author_session, created, and links
References binary content with a blob:// URI containing a hash
Separates machine metadata from a short human-readable source note in the body
concerns: Recording zero width and height for an image asset suggests missing or uncomputed dimensions
No links or contextual relationships are provided beyond the filename
The body label is terse and does not describe image content
notes: A minimal ledger stub that registers a recent-pitches JPEG as design-source ground truth, but omits dimensions, content description, and relationships.
questions: Why are the image dimensions recorded as 0?
What visual or conceptual content does p-31 contain?
How is this image used within the recent-pitches collection?
Does the blob hash include a verification algorithm or just the digest?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a source design ground-truth image; the actual content does that at the identity/provenance level but lacks dimensions, links, and content semantics.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a stable, traceable identity for a pitch image so the system can treat it as canonical design-source material.
novelty: Width and height are recorded as 0 for a JPEG entry, which is unusual for an image ledger record.
contribution: Provides a stable id, blob address, mime type, author session, and creation timestamp for a pitch image, giving a fused system a canonical reference point that can be enriched later with dimensions and relationships.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger metadata file for the design-source image recent-pitches/p-31, referencing a JPEG blob and declaring it as source design ground truth with zero dimensions and empty links.
intention_for_embedding: To register a pitch image as canonical, traceable design-source material within the channel-memory ledger.
